title: jasminum cinnamomifolium
intro: Jasminum cinnamomifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, commonly known as the Cinnamon Jasmine or Cinnamon Leaf Jasmine. It is a climbing shrub native to Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia, known for its fragrant white flowers and aromatic leaves that resemble the leaves of cinnamon trees. The leaves and flowers of Jasminum cinnamomifolium have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, have been used to promote digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and support immune function.
